headlines.title: US pivots to economic warfare on Iran with naval blockade and secondary sanctions on banks
S&P 500 closes at new record of 7,022.95, Nasdaq clears 24,000 on AI strength and easing war fears
Oil prices fall below $95 on hopes for renewed US-Iran peace talks
UK Chancellor Reeves calls US war on Iran a 'mistake' amid global economic fallout
Iran's economy faces collapse within three months under blockade, experts warn

notes: U.S. markets surged to record highs on April 15, driven by strong tech performance (Nasdaq > 24K) and optimism over potential U.S.-Iran negotiations.
The U.S. has enforced a naval blockade on Iranian ports, halting maritime trade, while Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced a pivot to economic warfare with secondary sanctions.
Brent crude dipped slightly below $95 per barrel amid shifting geopolitical risk premiums and hopes for a continued ceasefire.
